title: Query default routing and ready alias
description:
Make a bare query and the natural ready alias work instead of erroring.

## Background

Today `query` requires a subcommand and exposes `available` (cli.rs:1483), with no `ready` alias and no bare default, so the natural first guesses error. The shared `run_query_all` helper already exists (main.rs:689) with `--state`/`--assignee`/`--priority`/`--label` filters (main.rs:705-710; cli.rs:1458-1481) that can serve the default path.

## Success Criteria

- [hard] REQ-04: A bare jit query returns all issues via the existing query-all path, jit query ready is accepted as an alias of available, and the existing --state/--assignee/--priority/--label filters work unchanged on that default path with no new traversal semantic.

## Notes

Make the query subcommand optional so a bare `jit query` routes to `run_query_all` (main.rs:689, dispatched main.rs:3461-3470), and add `ready` as a visible alias of the existing `available` variant. `available` is retained, so no query name is retired, and there is no new traversal semantic. Update docs to mention `ready` and the bare default.
